source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Pipeline Pollution Event

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Main Pass Area Block 268

actions_taken: Upstream and downstream main line valves closed within 8 minutes
Pollution dome repositioned over largest subsea release point
National Response Center notified at 0123 hrs on 1 July 2022
BSEE notified at 0139 hrs on 1 July 2022
Damaged flange pieces removed and blind skillets installed
Aerial monitoring conducted multiple times daily
Full system integrity checks completed before restart
event.summary: On 30 June 2022 at 2346 hrs, while conducting subsea pipeline modification operations on PSN 11015 in the Gulf of Mexico (205 ft water depth, 41 miles offshore), a diamond wire saw cut through an 18-inch diameter pipeline. At approximately 65% completion of the cut, the pipeline suddenly separated due to unknown stored energy, causing each end to move in opposite directions. This unexpected torsion/tension caused the temporary 8-inch bypass piping to shear at a flange connection, releasing approximately 90 barrels of crude oil into the Gulf of Mexico over a 3-day period. The incident was caused by personnel not being aware of the amount of stored energy within the pipeline, and was contributed to by lack of communication with a pipeline contributor prior to performing the cut.
recommendations: All available historical information will be thoroughly reviewed to determine if potential for increase in stored energy exists on Third Coast pipelines under construction
Additional control measures will be implemented to mitigate substantial movement of pipeline prior to commencing construction work

threats.name: Bypass Piping Shear at Flange Connection
Inadequate Communication Prior to Pipeline Cut

consequences.name: Oil Discharge to Gulf of Mexico
Operational Shutdown and System Integrity Loss

controls.name: Hazard Assessment and Stored Energy Evaluation
controls.barrier_role: Identify and quantify stored energy in pipeline prior to cutting operations

supporting_text: Personnel were not aware of the amount of stored energy within the pipeline
operations did not account for or expect the amount of stored energy within the pipeline

controls.name: Pre-Cut Communication Protocol
controls.barrier_role: Ensure communication with all pipeline contributors before initiating cutting operations

supporting_text: Operator failed to confirm communication with a pipeline contributor prior to performing a pipeline cut

BSEE determined that the response time to shut in the MP 270 platform was inadequate. Panther should have confirmed communication with the operator of the platform prior to making the cut. This lack of communication caused significantly more pollution.

controls.name: Temporary Bypass Installation and Isolation
controls.barrier_role: Isolate pipeline section and provide alternative flow path during modification
controls.lod_basis: Engineering design and installation of bypass system

supporting_text: After installing an approximate 120-foot 8" diameter temporary bypass and purging the inactive section of 18" diameter pipe between the bypass linestops, the dive crew started to cut the 18" pipeline
With this sudden movement the 8" bypass piping and downstream hot tap BISEP line stop assembly twisted. Due to this unexpected torsion/tension, the temporary 8" by-pass sheared at a flange connection

controls.name: Real-Time Pressure Monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Detect pressure changes indicating loss of containment
controls.lod_basis: Continuous monitoring system at Third Coast Operations Control Center

supporting_text: At the time of the release the Third Coast Operations Control Center saw a significate pressure drop on the system, and immediately closed the upstream and downstream main line valves and contacted all producer platforms to additionally shut in

controls.name: Rapid Valve Isolation and Platform Shutdown
controls.barrier_role: Isolate affected pipeline section and stop production to minimize discharge volume
controls.lod_basis: Automated and manual valve closure procedures

supporting_text: All valves and producer platforms were shut-in within approximately 8 minutes

controls.name: Subsea Pollution Containment Dome
controls.barrier_role: Contain and recover discharged oil at source

supporting_text: The dive crew also responded by repositioning a previously deployed pollution dome over the largest subsea release point and were able to contain part of the release (27 barrels recovered)

controls.name: Incident Command System and Emergency Response
controls.barrier_role: Coordinate emergency response and minimize environmental impact
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response procedures and regulatory notification

supporting_text: An Incident Command System (ICS) was initiated, a command post established, and an Oil Spill Response Organization (OSRO) contacted and mobilized
Criteria for immediate notification to regulatory bodies was met, and a call was placed to the National Response Center (NRC) at 0123 hrs on July 1, 2022

controls.name: Post-Incident System Integrity Verification
controls.barrier_role: Verify pipeline integrity and prevent recurrence before resuming operations

supporting_text: The dive crews further inspected the line-stops (both upstream and downstream of the temporary by-pass); which continued to hold main line pressure without leaks. Similarly, all tie-in locations along the system were confirmed closed off and holding pressure without leaks
PSN 11015 remained shut in until July 12, 2022, at 1800 hrs after full system integrity checks were completed

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.

PIF-001.description: Personnel not aware of stored energy hazard
PIF-001.evidence: Personnel were not aware of the amount of stored energy within the pipeline

PIF-002.description: Inadequate training on stored energy hazards in pipeline modification
PIF-002.evidence: operations did not account for or expect the amount of stored energy within the pipeline

PIF-003.description: Failure to communicate with pipeline contributors before cutting
PIF-003.evidence: Operator failed to confirm communication with a pipeline contributor prior to performing a pipeline cut
